name: pi-workflows
description: Workflow orchestration extension for Pi. Adds composable, typed, multi-step workflow execution via .workflow.yaml specs. YAML specs define steps, typed JSON flows between them via ${{ }} expressions, each step spawns a pi subprocess with its own context window.
core_value: Composable, typed, multi-step workflow execution where the main conversation remains the control plane and workflows are subordinate work.
target_users: Pi users who need multi-step automated pipelines
Agent spec authors defining typed agent contracts
Extension developers building on the workflow runtime

constraints.description: Extension owns its own subprocess dispatch — independent of pi-subagents
Main conversation is the control plane; workflows are subordinate work
Each step runs as a spawned pi subprocess with its own context window
ESM module, TypeScript loaded directly by pi via --experimental-strip-types (no build step)
JSON is the intermediate representation between steps — file system is the data bus
Three template syntaxes: {{ var }}/{% tag %} (Nunjucks), ${{ expr }} (workflow data flow)
Domain lives in YAML specs, templates, and schemas — never in the runtime

in: Agent spec loading and prompt compilation
Sequential and parallel step execution via subprocess dispatch
Expression evaluation (${{ }} property access with pipe filters)
Nunjucks template rendering for prompt composition
TUI progress widgets and dashboard overlays
Step types: agent, gate, transform, loop, parallel, pause
Tool and command registration in pi
out: Domain-specific logic (belongs in YAML specs and templates)
Build step or transpilation (TypeScript stripped at runtime)
Budget enforcement ceilings (accumulation works, no gate)
